identity.name: Prisma Browser — Credential Paste into Untrusted Origin (T1552)
identity.summary: A managed user navigates the Prisma Browser to a non-sanctioned phishing origin and pastes a corporate-SSO-shaped secret into the page's password field. Prisma Browser DLP inspects the clipboard/keystroke payload at paste time, recognizes the credential pattern, and fires because the destination origin is not on the sanctioned allowlist — the canonical browser data-loss vector for credential ph…
identity.description: Credential phishing succeeds at the moment a user types or pastes a real secret into an attacker-controlled page. Prisma Browser closes that gap by enforcing DLP inside the rendering context itself: it sees the paste/keystroke content and the destination origin before the form is submitted, so a secret-shaped string (`MyCorpSSO!@#-CORTEXSIM-CANARY-2026`, carrying canary `CANARY-CRED-001`) landing in `input[name="password"]` on `login.cortexsim-test.invalid` — an origin absent from the sanctioned SaaS allowlist — trips the credential-paste DLP rule. The cortex-browser-attacker EAL plugin drives a real Chromium/Prisma Browser session through navigate → paste actions, emitting `prisma_browser_events` telemetry with `action=paste`, `origin`, `url`, `data_classification`, and the canary marker. Detection layers: a BIOC matches the credential-shape paste into a non-sanctioned origin; an analytics query flags the paste-source vs. destination-origin mismatch; a delivery-path query confirms the alert reached XSIAM. A correlation rule fuses the BIOC and origin-mismatch analytic into one DLP incident.

prerequisites.description: Managed Prisma Browser deployed and reporting telemetry to XSIAM.
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe browser_attack_runner | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-BROWSER-001 (cortex-browser-attacker installed; Playwright Chromium/Prisma channel available).

payload.code:
# Drives the managed Prisma Browser through the cred-paste campaign.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/sim-browser-001-eal.yml --live
# Campaign: navigate https://login.cortexsim-test.invalid/signin
#           paste "MyCorpSSO!@#-CORTEXSIM-CANARY-2026" into input[name="password"]
#           cortex_canary: CANARY-CRED-001

expected_artifacts.description: Paste action into a password field on a non-sanctioned origin carrying the credential canary.
Navigation to a non-sanctioned origin immediately preceding the paste.

biocs.name: Prisma Browser — credential-shape paste into non-sanctioned origin (DLP rule)
biocs.description: Prisma Browser — credential-shape paste into non-sanctioned origin (DLP rule). A paste action carrying a credential-classified payload lands in a password/secret field on an origin that is not on the sanctioned SaaS allowlist.
biocs.logic:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter action = "paste"
| filter data_classification in ("credential", "secret", "password")
| filter origin not in ("login.corp.example.com", "okta.corp.example.com", "login.microsoftonline.com")
| filter target_selector contains "password" or url contains "signin" or url contains "login"

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: plugin metadata visible to operator
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter campaign_id = "BC-BROWSER-001"
| filter action in ("navigate", "paste")
| fields _time, campaign_id, action, origin, url, user
| sort asc _time

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — paste source vs. destination origin mismatch
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "paste"
| filter data_classification in ("credential", "secret", "password")
| filter origin not in ("login.corp.example.com", "okta.corp.example.com", "login.microsoftonline.com")
| fields _time, user, origin, url, action, data_classification, cortex_canary
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: PB → XSIAM — alert with origin URL + paste DLP rule fires within MTTD target
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "paste" and cortex_canary = "CANARY-CRED-001"
| fields _time, user, origin, url, dlp_rule_name, data_classification, cortex_canary
| sort desc _time

correlation_rules.name: Browser Credential Paste DLP — Non-Sanctioned Origin
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Prisma Browser — credential-shape paste into non-sanctioned origin (DLP rule))

analytics_modules: Prisma Browser DLP — Credential pattern inspection at paste time
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Sanctioned-origin allowlist deviation

products.evidence_query: dataset = prisma_browser_events | filter action = "paste" and data_classification = "credential" | fields _time, user, origin, url
Correlation CR-BROWSER-0001 raises a DLP incident when a credential-shape paste hits a non-sanctioned origin.

use_cases.name: Browser DLP — Credential Disclosure
use_cases.description: Detect and block corporate-secret-shaped data pasted into non-sanctioned origins inside the managed Prisma Browser.

test_cases.objective: Credential-shape paste into a non-sanctioned origin fires the Prisma Browser DLP BIOC.
preconditions: Managed Prisma Browser reporting to XSIAM.
PB DLP credential-paste rule imported and enabled.
steps: Observe the DLP alert in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `Prisma Browser — credential-shape paste into non-sanctioned origin (DLP rule)` fires within 60s.
All three XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.
Alert carries origin URL and canary CANARY-CRED-001.

test_cases.objective: Origin-mismatch analytic and BIOC stitch into one incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-BROWSER-0001` produces a single DLP incident.

preventive_controls: Enable Prisma Browser DLP credential-pattern inspection in Block mode for non-sanctioned origins.
Maintain a tight sanctioned-SaaS allowlist and treat all other origins as untrusted for secret entry.
Deploy phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) so a pasted password alone is not sufficient to authenticate.
detection_engineering: Alert on any paste classified as credential/secret into an origin outside the sanctioned allowlist.
Correlate the paste origin against the corporate IdP origin to confirm phishing intent.
Hunt for repeated credential-shape pastes from the same user across multiple untrusted origins.
